name: Kentucky Bourbon Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate raisins in 1 cup of the bourbon for at least 1 hour, up to 12 hours. Drain and reserve both the raisins and bourbon.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
3. Butter and flour a 10-inch Bundt cake pan or other tube-cake pan.
4. Beat together butter and sugar until light and fluffy using an electric mixer, starting at slow speed and gradually increasing.
5. If time allows, the process can take about 15 minutes, resulting in a very pale butter that resembles whipped cream.
6. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
7. In another bowl, sift together 3 3/4 cups flour, baking powder, soda, and salt.
8. Combine reserved bourbon with lemon zest and lemon juice.
9. Add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture alternately with the bourbon mixture.
10. Toss the remaining 1/4 cup of flour with the drained raisins and pecans. Fold these into the batter.
11. Bake for about 1 1/2 hours or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
12. Cool for about 10 minutes in the pan before turning out onto a rack.
13. Brush the remaining 1/2 cup of bourbon over the cake while it is still warm.
14. For long storage, wrap the cake in rinsed cheesecloth that has been thoroughly moistened with bourbon, then wrap in plastic to keep it airtight. The cake can be served after a day or two, but is better if allowed to age.
